title: Align lockfile input with supported Python versions
outOfScope: Unrelated dependency upgrades or unrelated lockfile tool behavior changes.
Release publication, tags, provider assets, and adopter-project installation.

sources.reason: The exact stevedore pin is not installable on the supported Python 3.10 lockfile lane.
Committed lock output currently records the Python 3.10-compatible stevedore version.
The committed release evidence binds the supply-chain lock digest and must remain consistent with the corrected lock output.
The reproducibility gate must compare semantic lock output rather than environment-specific pip-tools comment grouping.
The provider lane must use the same pinned pip-tools compiler as the canonical lockfile process.
Regression coverage must keep input and lock output aligned for the supported baseline.
Regression coverage must keep lockfile normalization and executable resolution as hard preflight behavior.
Provider evidence shows stevedore==5.9.0 cannot be resolved on Python 3.10.

riskAssessment.reason: An input pin unsupported by the lowest supported Python version makes the reproducibility gate impossible to pass.

executionDecision.reason: The provider failure identifies a deterministic dependency-input correction.

preReviewWarnings: Verify the input pin, committed lock output, and Python 3.10 provider gate remain aligned.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The Python 3.10 lockfile reproducibility lane passes after the stevedore pin correction, using a pinned pip-tools compiler and the CI-generated lock output.
release.json requirementsLockDigest matches the committed lockfile without publishing a release.

intent.problem: The dependency input requests stevedore==5.9.0 while the supported Python 3.10 reproducibility lane cannot install that release; the committed lock output remains at 5.8.0.
constraints: Do not broaden this Work Item into unrelated dependency updates.
Keep publication frozen until the lockfile gate passes in provider CI.
intent.rationale: Dependency inputs must describe selections that the lowest supported execution environment can actually resolve and reproduce.

rawUserRequest: When CI or PR exposes a process defect, stop publication and fix the root workflow first. Do not bypass the failure or create a budget-only Work Item.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Python 3.10 lockfile CI resolves the input and reproduces the committed lock output.
scenarioCoverage.reason: Provider CI must verify after push.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized root-cause dependency compatibility correction before resuming release; archive budget is recorded in this functional Work Item.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
